hero.title: 11 Days, Sacred Trails.
hero.titleAccent: The Great Pilgrimage Circuit of the North.
hero.intro: A deep spiritual journey through the holiest sites of North India. Varanasi, Prayagraj, Ayodhya, Haridwar and Rishikesh, the sacred cities of the Ganges and the gateway to the Himalayas. Eleven days of devotion and sacred geography.
hero.heroImageAlt: Sacred trails of India Varanasi Haridwar Rishikesh pilgrimage

overview.heading: 11 Days Sacred Trails of India: The Full Picture
body: This 11 day pilgrimage is the most complete spiritual circuit of North India, linking the great sacred cities of the Ganges plains and the Himalayan foothills. From Varanasi's eternal ghats and Prayagraj's sacred confluence to Ayodhya's Ram Mandir and the Ganga aartis of Haridwar and Rishikesh, it is a profound journey through the spiritual heart of the country.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip is designed for devotees and seekers who want time and depth. A professional guide and a private vehicle link the cities, with sunrise boats, evening aartis, temple darshans and the serene yoga capital of Rishikesh. It is a slow, meaningful immersion in sacred India.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Varanasi & the Sacred Triangle
items.body: Varanasi's ghats and Ganga Aarti, Prayagraj's Triveni Sangam, and Ayodhya's Ram Mandir, the great sacred triangle of the Ganges plains.

items.title: Haridwar's Ganga Aarti
items.body: The spectacular evening Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri in Haridwar, where the Ganges enters the plains, one of the most moving ceremonies in India.
items.imageAlt: Haridwar Ganga Aarti Har Ki Pauri

items.title: Rishikesh, Yoga Capital
items.body: The serene Himalayan-foothill town of Rishikesh, with its ashrams, the Lakshman Jhula bridge, the sound of the Ganges, and the gentle spirituality of the yoga capital of the world.

days.title: Ayodhya to Lucknow to Delhi

days.title: Delhi to Haridwar

suggestedActivities: Har Ki Pauri evening Ganga Aarti

days.title: Rishikesh Sightseeing

whyBook.heading: The Great Pilgrimage Circuit, Guided
whyBook.body: Linking the sacred cities of the plains and the foothills takes careful routing and a guide who understands each tradition. We handle the boats, the aartis and the darshans. Dhesu has arranged spiritual India journeys since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Sacred Trails of India Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 11 day pilgrimage is the most complete spiritual circuit of North India, linking Varanasi, Prayagraj, Ayodhya, Haridwar and Rishikesh, the great sacred cities of the Ganges.

With a professional guide and a private vehicle, you experience sunrise boats, evening aartis, temple darshans and the serene yoga capital of Rishikesh, a slow, meaningful journey.

It is ideal for devotees and seekers who want depth and time, rather than a quick highlights tour.

sections.heading: From the Ganges Plains to the Foothills
sections.body:
This journey follows the sacred geography of the Ganges, from Varanasi where it flows through the holiest of cities, to Prayagraj's confluence, and up to Haridwar and Rishikesh where the river enters the plains from the Himalayas.

The Ganga aartis at Varanasi, Haridwar and Rishikesh, each with its own character, are highlights, alongside the temples of Ayodhya and the ashrams of the yoga capital.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Varanasi and Dehradun Airports (VNS / DED). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for this pilgrimage?
items.answer: October to March offers the most comfortable weather across the plains and foothills. Rishikesh and Haridwar are pleasant most of the year, though winter mornings are cold. Festivals and the Kumbh Mela bring immense crowds at certain times.

items.question: How physically demanding is this trip?
items.answer: It involves significant driving and a lot of temple visits, ghats and steps, but no trekking. The eleven-day pace keeps it manageable, and the private vehicle makes the longer drives comfortable. A reasonable level of mobility is helpful.

items.question: Can I include yoga or meditation in Rishikesh?
items.answer: Yes. Rishikesh is the yoga capital of the world, and we can arrange yoga or meditation sessions at an ashram during your stay. Let us know your interest when booking so we can include it.

cta.heading: Ready for the sacred trails?
